failure_modes.why: Blue churned dozens of 3:1 maritime trades burning resources without converting to enough builds or VP.
Blue's economy relied on constant ore/wood trades rather than balanced pip production, leaving it short on real resources.
Blue stalled at 5-7 VP with no dev-card army or longest-road plan while Red bought cards and expanded to 10.

review_summary: Blue over-traded a thin production engine into a dead end, never building a coherent dev-card/expansion path to 10 while Red converted steadily.

13.summary: A solid robber placement on a RED tile that steals and disrupts with zero regret.

0.summary: A defensible opening settlement with port access that ties the oracle's best legal spot (regret 0.00).

57.summary: A defensible expansion road with zero regret versus the oracle's alternative, just not directly VP-generating.

112.summary: Optimal robber placement on Blue's productive tile, identical to the oracle's best legal move.

failed.claude: BUILD_SETTLEMENT 53 was legal and gives an immediate VP, whereas this road just sets up a future settlement and wastes a 3:1 trade on top.
failed.openai: Building a settlement at 53 would have immediately increased VP, which is a higher tempo move.

failed.claude: Spending wood for a road plus a maritime trade for brick yields no VP when settling node 53 directly would have delivered a VP from a similar spend.
failed.openai: Building a settlement at 53 would have provided a direct VP gain for the same resources.

167.summary: BLUE built a road and burned a trade when BUILD_SETTLEMENT 53 was legal and would have immediately scored a VP, costing real tempo and efficiency.

211.summary: A defensible robber placement on an opponent's productive tile with negligible regret while RED is behind.

249.summary: A reasonable bank trade converting surplus wood toward a build, defensible despite the slightly indirect ore->brick path the oracle would skip.

313.summary: A reasonable 3:1 wheat-surplus trade for needed brick that wastes nothing and matches oracle's near-zero regret.

275.summary: Sensible 3:1 dump of surplus sheep for needed brick to enable a road build, fully defensible.

131.summary: A defensible but inefficient 3:1 ore-for-wheat trade that doesn't complete a build; buying a dev card with the surplus ore would have been better.

341.summary: A sound bank trade converting excess wheat into building materials toward a needed road, matching the oracle's zero-regret line.

359.summary: A reasonable 3:1 surplus-wood-to-brick bank trade with near-zero regret, only slightly behind the oracle's wheat preference.

399.summary: RED correctly robs the trailing opponent's settlement, matching the oracle's best move while leading and one VP from victory.

419.summary: RED's road build is a sound, zero-regret endgame move to extend longest road and close out the win.

455.summary: Solid endgame robber placement that blocks Blue and steals toward RED's winning VP with no regret.
